units.title: The family
units.topic: Family members and relationships

words.description: A woman who has children; the female parent
words.example: My mother works as a teacher.

words.description: A man who has children; the male parent
words.example: My father is a doctor.

words.description: A male child in relation to his parents
words.example: They have one son and two daughters.

words.description: A female child in relation to her parents
words.example: Karen is Anne and Ivan's daughter.

words.description: A boy or man with the same parents as you
words.example: Jack is Karen's brother.

words.description: A girl or woman with the same parents as you
words.example: Karen is Jack's sister.

words.description: A man who is married; the male partner in a marriage
words.example: Ivan is Anne's husband.

words.description: A woman who is married; the female partner in a marriage
words.example: Anne is Ivan's wife.

words.description: Your mother and father together
words.example: Anne and Ivan are Karen and Jack's parents.

words.description: The sons and daughters of a person
words.example: They have two children: Karen and Jack.

words.description: The father of your mother or father
words.example: Henry is Karen and Jack's grandfather.

words.description: The mother of your mother or father
words.example: Diana is their grandmother.

words.description: The parents of your mother or father
words.example: Henry and Diana are the grandparents.

words.description: The son of your son or daughter
words.example: Jack is Henry and Diana's grandson.

words.description: The daughter of your son or daughter
words.example: Karen is Henry and Diana's granddaughter.

words.description: The brother of your mother or father
words.example: George is Karen and Jack's uncle.

words.description: The sister of your mother or father
words.example: Amelia and Meena are Karen and Jack's aunts.

words.description: The son of your brother or sister
words.example: Jack is their nephew.

words.description: The daughter of your brother or sister
words.example: Karen is their niece.

words.description: The child of your aunt or uncle
words.example: Kavita and Ravi are Karen and Jack's cousins.

words.description: Members of your family; people related to you
words.example: We have relatives living in many different cities.

words.description: A child who has no brothers or sisters
words.example: Have you got any brothers and sisters? No, I am an only child.

units.title: Birth, marriage and death
units.topic: Life events: being born, getting married, and dying

words.description: To give birth to a child
words.example: Anna had a baby yesterday.

words.description: To come into the world at birth
words.example: He was born at 1.15 yesterday morning.

words.description: To have a particular weight
words.example: The baby weighed 3 kilograms.

words.description: The anniversary of the day you were born
words.example: His grandfather's birthday is June 16th.

words.description: To give someone a particular name
words.example: They are going to call him John, after his grandfather.

words.description: Not married; without a partner
words.example: If you do not have a partner, you are single.

words.description: Having a husband or wife
words.example: If you have a husband or wife, you are married.

words.description: To become husband and wife in a ceremony
words.example: Harry and Sarah got married in 2001.

words.description: A ceremony where two people get married
words.example: The wedding was a beautiful ceremony.

words.description: A woman on her wedding day
words.example: The bride wore a beautiful white dress.

words.description: A man on his wedding day
words.example: The groom waited nervously at the altar.

words.description: A holiday taken by a newly married couple after the wedding
words.example: They went on their honeymoon to Italy.

words.description: Having lost your husband or wife through death
words.example: If your husband or wife dies, you are widowed.

words.description: Living apart from your husband or wife but not yet divorced
words.example: If your marriage breaks up, you are separated or divorced.

words.description: No longer legally married
words.example: The marriage has legally ended — they are divorced.

words.description: Not well; sick
words.example: Then Harry became ill.

words.description: To stop living
words.example: He died last year.

words.description: To die as a result of a particular cause or illness
words.example: He died of a heart attack.

words.description: No longer alive. Note: say 'Harry is dead' NOT 'Harry is died'
words.example: Harry is dead.

words.description: A ceremony held after someone dies before burial or cremation
words.example: The funeral was attended by many friends and family.

words.description: A sudden serious medical condition where the heart stops working normally
words.example: My father had a heart attack last year.

units.title: Parts of the body
units.topic: Names of the main parts of the human body

words.description: The thin threads growing from the skin on your head. Used as singular: 'My hair is long' NOT 'My hairs are long'
words.example: My hair is very long — I must cut it soon.

words.description: One of the two organs in your face that you use to see
words.example: She has blue eyes.

words.description: The part of your face above your mouth used for smelling and breathing
words.example: He broke his nose playing football.

words.description: The hard white objects in your mouth. Singular: tooth, plural: teeth
words.example: Brush your teeth twice a day.

words.description: The opening in your face used for eating and speaking
words.example: Open your mouth and say 'ah'.

words.description: One of the two organs on the sides of your head that you use to hear
words.example: She wore earrings on her ears.

words.description: Either of the two soft edges around the opening of the mouth
words.example: He has thin lips.

words.description: The part of the body connecting the head to the shoulders
words.example: She wore a scarf around her neck.

words.description: The joint connecting the arm to the upper body
words.example: He carried the bag over his shoulder.

words.description: The hard thin covering at the end of your fingers and toes
words.example: She painted her nails red.

words.description: The short thick finger at the side of your hand, separate from the other four
words.example: He gave a thumbs-up to say everything was OK.

words.description: One of the four long thin parts at the end of your hand
words.example: She wore a ring on her finger.

words.description: The part at the end of your arm including fingers and thumb
words.example: Wash your hands before eating.

words.description: The long part of your body from shoulder to hand
words.example: She broke her arm in the accident.

words.description: One of the two long parts of your body used for walking
words.example: She hurt her leg playing sport.

words.description: The joint in the middle of the leg that allows it to bend
words.example: She hurt her knee while running.

words.description: The part at the bottom of your leg that you stand on. Singular: foot, plural: feet
words.example: My feet are sore after walking all day.

words.description: One of the five parts at the end of your foot
words.example: He broke his toe.

words.description: The front of the body between the neck and the stomach; contains the heart and lungs
words.example: The doctor listened to his chest with a stethoscope.

words.description: The organ in your body that digests food; also the area below the chest
words.example: I have a pain in my stomach.

words.description: The rear part of your body from shoulders to hips
words.example: He has a bad back from sitting all day.

words.description: Either of the two parts of the body between the chest and the hip
words.example: I have a pain in my side.

words.description: The narrow part of your body between your chest and hips
words.example: She has a very slim waist.

words.description: The area on each side of the body between the waist and the top of the leg
words.example: She fell and broke her hip.

words.description: The outer covering of the human body
words.example: We have skin covering our bodies.

words.description: The organ inside your chest that pumps blood around your body
words.example: The heart beats about 70 times a minute.

words.description: The organ inside your head that controls thought, memory and feeling
words.example: The human brain is very complex.

words.description: The red liquid that flows through your body
words.example: He lost a lot of blood in the accident.

units.title: Clothes
units.topic: Types of clothing, accessories, and verbs for wearing clothes

words.description: A long warm piece of clothing worn over other clothes outdoors
words.example: Take a coat — it will be cold tonight.

words.description: A short coat with sleeves, worn over other clothes
words.example: She wore a green jacket to the meeting.

words.description: A piece of clothing for the upper body with a collar and buttons
words.example: He always wears a white shirt to work.

words.description: A simple shirt with short sleeves and no collar or buttons
words.example: He wore a blue T-shirt and jeans.

words.description: A warm knitted piece of clothing for the upper body
words.example: She put on a sweater because it was cold.

words.description: A one-piece garment for women covering the body from shoulders downward
words.example: She bought a beautiful red dress for the wedding.

words.description: A piece of clothing worn by women that hangs from the waist down
words.example: She wore a long skirt to the party.

words.description: A piece of clothing covering the lower body with separate legs; 'pants' in American English. Always plural
words.example: He wore black trousers to the interview.

words.description: Trousers made from denim. Always plural
words.example: She always wears jeans at the weekend.

words.description: Short trousers ending above or at the knee. Always plural
words.example: He wore shorts on the beach.

words.description: A tight covering for the legs and lower body, worn by women. Always plural
words.example: She wore black tights with her skirt.

words.description: A set of matching jacket and trousers worn for formal occasions
words.example: He wore a dark blue suit to the job interview.

words.description: A covering worn on the head, usually with a brim
words.example: She wore a big red hat to the party.

words.description: A long piece of cloth worn around the neck or head for warmth or style
words.example: He wrapped a scarf around his neck on the cold day.

words.description: Coverings for the hands with separate parts for each finger. Always plural
words.example: Don't forget your gloves — it's freezing outside.

words.description: Coverings for the feet. Always plural
words.example: She bought new shoes for the party.

words.description: Comfortable sports shoes; called 'sneakers' in American English
words.example: I wear trainers when I go to the gym.

words.description: Strong shoes that cover the foot and ankle or higher up the leg
words.example: She wears boots in winter to keep her feet warm.

words.description: Soft coverings for your feet, worn inside shoes. Always plural
words.example: He always wears white socks.

words.description: A strip of leather or cloth worn around the waist to hold up trousers
words.example: His trousers were too big, so he wore a belt.

words.description: A long piece of cloth worn around the neck, usually by men for formal occasions
words.example: He wore a tie to the business meeting.

words.description: A small clock worn on the wrist
words.example: He looked at his watch and realised he was late.

words.description: A circular piece of jewellery worn on a finger
words.example: She wore a gold ring on her left hand.

words.description: Lenses in a frame worn over the eyes to help you see better. Always plural
words.example: She wears glasses for reading.

words.description: Dark glasses worn to protect your eyes from bright sunlight. Always plural
words.example: Don't forget your sunglasses at the beach.

words.description: A bag carried by women for personal belongings
words.example: She's carrying a suitcase and a handbag.

words.description: A device with a circular canopy on a stick used as protection from rain
words.example: You carry a bag and an umbrella.

words.description: To have clothing or accessories on your body. Note: you WEAR clothes but CARRY bags
words.example: Naomi is wearing a long blue coat.

words.description: To place clothing on your body. Note: 'put clothes ON' not 'put off'
words.example: In the morning you get dressed or put your clothes on.

words.description: To remove clothing from your body. Note: 'take clothes OFF' not 'take off clothes'
words.example: At night you get undressed or take your clothes off.

units.title: Describing people
units.topic: Words for describing height, weight, hair, face and age

words.description: Having greater than average height. Note: say 'People are tall' NOT 'People are high'
words.example: Bettina Schwenke is a very tall woman.

words.description: Having less than average height
words.example: Tom Jakes is quite short.

words.description: Neither tall nor short; average height
words.example: If you aren't tall or short, you are of medium height.

words.description: Thin in an attractive, healthy way. More polite than 'thin'
words.example: Agata Sanchez is really slim.

words.description: Having little fat on the body; not fat
words.example: I was very thin when I was in hospital.

words.description: Heavier than is considered healthy
words.example: The doctor said I am overweight.

words.description: Having too much flesh on the body. Note: it is not polite to say this to someone
words.example: Their cat is very fat. It needs to go on a diet.

words.description: Having a darker skin colour
words.example: Suri has dark skin and dark hair.

words.description: Having a light, pale skin colour
words.example: Polly has fair skin and blonde hair.

words.description: Light yellow coloured hair. Note: say 'blonde/dark hair' NOT 'blonde/dark hairs'
words.example: Polly has blonde hair and fair skin.

words.description: Black or very dark brown hair
words.example: Suri has dark skin and dark hair.

words.description: Hair growing on a man's chin and cheeks
words.example: Ben has a beard and long hair.

words.description: Hair grown on the upper lip
words.example: Luca has a moustache and short hair.

words.description: Very attractive; usually used for women. 'My mother is a very beautiful woman'
words.example: My mother is a very beautiful woman.

words.description: Attractive in appearance; usually used for men
words.example: My dad's a very good-looking man.

words.description: Attractive; usually used for girls or women
words.example: My sister is very pretty.

words.description: Not attractive; the opposite of beautiful or good-looking
words.example: Bob's an ugly man.

words.description: Neither attractive nor unattractive; ordinary looking
words.example: I'm not ugly or beautiful, I'm just average-looking!

words.description: Having lived for a long time; not young
words.example: My grandmother is 97. She's very old.

words.description: Having lived for a short time; not old
words.example: My sister is 14. She's young.

words.description: Between about 40 and 60 years old
words.example: My father is 56. He's middle-aged.

words.description: Old; a polite word for old people
words.example: This hospital is for elderly people.

units.title: Health and illness
units.topic: Words for health conditions, common illnesses, and wellbeing

words.description: In a very healthy and good state
words.example: I'm very well, thanks.

words.description: Feeling good and healthy
words.example: I'm fine, thanks.

words.description: To feel slightly ill or not fully healthy
words.example: I don't feel very well. I must go home and rest.

words.description: To feel sick with an illness
words.example: I feel ill. Can you get a doctor, please?

words.description: Feeling like you want to vomit; also used to mean ill in American English
words.example: That fish was bad. I think I'm going to be sick!

words.description: A continuous pain in your head
words.example: Have you got an aspirin? I've got a headache.

words.description: A pain in one or more of your teeth
words.example: I've got toothache. I need to go to the dentist.

words.description: A common illness causing a runny nose and sore throat
words.example: I'm going to bed with a hot drink. I've got a cold.

words.description: A doctor who treats your teeth and mouth
words.example: I've got toothache. I need to go to the dentist.

words.description: An allergy to pollen causing sneezing and watery eyes
words.example: I get hay fever every summer from flowers and grass.

words.description: To suddenly blow air out of your nose and mouth uncontrollably
words.example: I sneeze all day because of hay fever.

words.description: A condition that makes it difficult to breathe
words.example: My little brother has asthma; sometimes he can't breathe.

words.description: A small flying insect that bites and can spread diseases
words.example: In some countries, mosquitoes can give people malaria.

words.description: A serious disease spread by mosquitoes in tropical countries
words.example: The drinking water was bad, and many children had cholera.

words.description: A serious disease caused by cells growing in an uncontrolled way
words.example: Every year cancer kills many people who smoke.

words.description: A building where sick or injured people receive medical treatment
words.example: My father went to hospital when he had a heart attack.

words.description: A sudden serious condition where the heart stops working normally. Note: 'had a heart attack' NOT 'got a heart attack'
words.example: My father had a heart attack.

words.description: To eat food that is good for your health
words.example: Do you have a healthy diet? Yes, I eat lots of fruit and vegetables.

words.description: Physical activity done to keep the body fit and healthy
words.example: Do you exercise? Yes, I like swimming, jogging and cycling.

words.description: Beneficial for your health
words.example: Swimming, jogging and cycling are really good for you.

words.description: Feeling worried and anxious because of problems or too much work
words.example: I feel stressed! I need to relax more.

words.description: To become calm and free from stress
words.example: You need to relax more and don't panic about work.

units.title: Feelings
units.topic: Words for emotions, preferences and desires

words.description: To feel very strong liking or affection; the strongest positive feeling (++)
words.example: I love my family and my best friend.

words.description: To find something pleasant or enjoyable (+)
words.example: I like my job.

words.description: To not enjoy or find something pleasant (-)
words.example: I don't like horror films.

words.description: To have a very strong feeling of dislike (--)
words.example: I hate spiders.

words.description: Feeling pleasure and satisfaction; not sad. Note: 'happy about' or 'happy for' NOT 'happy for'
words.example: I am very happy about your news.

words.description: Feeling unhappy or sorrowful
words.example: He was sad when his dog died.

words.description: Feeling or showing strong displeasure
words.example: She was very angry when she heard the news.

words.description: Feeling unhappy and worried because something bad has happened
words.example: He was upset when his friend didn't call.

words.description: Feeling a low temperature; needing warmth
words.example: I'm cold. Can you turn the heating on?

words.description: Feeling very warm; feeling too much heat
words.example: I'm hot. Can you open a window?

words.description: Feeling the need to drink water or other liquids
words.example: I'm thirsty. Can I have some water, please?

words.description: Feeling the need to eat food
words.example: I'm hungry. When is dinner?

words.description: In good health; feeling healthy
words.example: He's very well, thanks.

words.description: Not feeling healthy; sick
words.example: She looks ill. She should see a doctor.

words.description: Feeling a need to rest or sleep; lacking energy
words.example: I'm very tired. I need to sleep.

words.description: Feeling astonishment at something unexpected
words.example: She was surprised when she saw him at the party.

words.description: To like one thing more than another
words.example: I prefer coffee to tea. (= I like coffee more than I like tea.)

words.description: To want something to happen and believe it might. Note: 'I hope to do' or 'I hope that' NOT 'I hope doing'
words.example: I hope to do well in my exam.

words.description: To feel a desire for something. Note: 'I want you to help me' NOT 'I want that you help me'
words.example: I want a new car. I want to buy a new car.

units.title: Conversations 1: Greetings and wishes
units.topic: Everyday greetings, farewells, and special occasion expressions

words.description: A greeting used from waking up until midday (about 12pm)
words.example: Good morning! How are you today?

words.description: A greeting used from midday until about 6pm
words.example: Good afternoon, can I help you?

words.description: A greeting used in the evening, from about 6pm onwards
words.example: Good evening, welcome to the restaurant.

words.description: What you say when you leave someone or end a conversation
words.example: Goodbye! See you tomorrow.

words.description: An informal farewell used when you expect to see someone again soon
words.example: Goodbye! See you soon!

words.description: What you say when someone goes to bed. Note: only say goodnight when LEAVING, not when arriving somewhere
words.example: Goodnight! Sleep well.

words.description: Used when making a polite request
words.example: Can I have a coffee, please?

words.description: What you say when someone does something nice for you
words.example: Thank you for your help.

words.description: An informal British way to say thank you
words.example: Cheers! Thanks for buying the coffee.

words.description: Used to politely get someone's attention or to pass by someone
words.example: Excuse me, can you tell me the way to the station?

words.description: Used to apologise when you have done something wrong
words.example: Sorry! I didn't mean to bump into you.

words.description: What you say when someone sneezes
words.example: Achoo! — Bless you! — Thank you.

words.description: What you say to someone on their birthday. Note: say 'Happy Birthday' NOT 'Congratulations'
words.example: Happy Birthday! How old are you today?

words.description: What you say at Christmas time
words.example: Happy Christmas! Are you spending it with family?

words.description: What you say on or around New Year's Day
words.example: Happy New Year! I hope this year is a good one.

words.description: What you say when someone is about to do something difficult like an exam
words.example: Good luck with your exam tomorrow!

words.description: What you say when someone has achieved something special or done something well in an exam or had a baby
words.example: Congratulations! I heard you passed your exam.

words.description: What you say to praise someone who has done something well
words.example: Well done! That was an excellent presentation.

units.title: Conversations 2: Useful words and expressions
units.topic: Common words and expressions used in everyday conversation

words.description: In reality; used to say what is really true. Note: in English means 'in reality' NOT 'now' — it is a false friend in some languages
words.example: People say bad things about her, but she's actually very nice.

words.description: Very; used to emphasise something
words.example: The book is really good.

words.description: In addition to or different from what has been said
words.example: Do you want to buy anything else? Or go somewhere else?

words.description: Approximately; about a particular number or time
words.example: I'll meet you at around 6 o'clock.

words.description: Used to give a reason, or to return to an earlier subject in conversation
words.example: I'll drive you home anyway. — Anyway, as I said, I woke up very late today.

words.description: Used to make a suggestion to a group. Note: 'Why don't we go...' NOT 'Why don't we going...'
words.example: Why don't we go to the cinema this evening?

words.description: Used to suggest doing something together. Note: 'Let's go...' NOT 'Let's going...'
words.example: Let's go and see a film and then have a meal.

words.description: Used to make a suggestion or ask someone's opinion. Note: 'How about going...' NOT 'How about go...'
words.example: How about the new Angelina Jolie film?

words.description: It is the same to me; I have no preference
words.example: I don't mind. It's up to you.

words.description: You can decide; it is your choice
words.example: I don't mind. It's up to you.

words.uzbek: Voy! Ne balo! (hayrat yoki xafa bo'lganda)
words.description: Used when you are surprised or disappointed about something
words.example: Oh dear! Was your boss angry?

words.description: It is not important; don't worry about it
words.example: I forgot to bring your book. Oh, it doesn't matter. I don't need it.

words.description: Used when you are disappointed or sorry about something
words.example: I'm sorry, but I can't come to your party. What a pity!

words.description: Used to tell someone to be quick
words.example: Hurry up! The taxi's here.

words.description: Used to warn someone about danger
words.example: Look out! There's a car coming.

words.description: Used when you agree strongly with a suggestion or statement
words.example: Absolutely! What about getting her a scarf?

words.uzbek: Roziman, qo'shilaman. Note: 'I agree' NOT 'I am agree'
words.description: Used to say you have the same opinion. Note: NOT 'I am agree'
words.example: I agree. Let's go shopping this afternoon.

words.description: Used to praise someone who has done something well
words.example: I passed my exam! Well done!

units.title: Food and drink
units.topic: Common food items, fast food, fruit, vegetables, and drinks

words.description: A basic food made from flour and water, baked in an oven. Note: 'some bread' NOT 'a bread'
words.example: Would you like some bread?

words.description: Small white or brown grains cooked and eaten as a staple food
words.example: I love sushi because I love rice.

words.description: An Italian food made from flour and water in various shapes such as spaghetti
words.example: Pasta is good for you, but don't eat too much!

words.description: A white mineral used to flavour food
words.example: I always put salt on my chips, but not pepper.

words.description: A hot spicy powder used to flavour food
words.example: I always put salt on my chips, but not pepper.

words.description: The flesh of animals eaten as food, such as beef, chicken or lamb
words.example: My sister never eats meat or fish. She's vegetarian.

words.description: A sea or river animal used as food
words.example: My sister never eats meat or fish.

words.description: A person who does not eat meat or fish
words.example: She's vegetarian, so she doesn't eat meat or fish.

words.description: A sweet white or brown substance used to sweeten food and drinks
words.example: Do you take sugar in tea or coffee?

words.description: A hot drink made from dried leaves of the tea plant
words.example: Do you take sugar in your tea?

words.description: A hot drink made from roasted coffee beans
words.example: I have a cup of coffee every morning.

words.description: A cooked patty of minced beef served in a bread roll
words.example: I eat hamburgers when I don't have much time.

words.description: A cooked sausage served in a long bread roll
words.example: I eat hot dogs and pizzas when I don't have much time.

words.description: An Italian dish of flat bread covered with tomato, cheese and toppings
words.example: I eat hamburgers, hot dogs and pizzas when I don't have much time.

words.description: A traditional British meal of fried fish with fried potato chips
words.example: Fish and chips is popular in Britain, Australia and New Zealand.

words.description: The sweet product of a plant. Note: 'fruit' is uncountable — 'I love fruit' NOT 'I love fruits'
words.example: Fruit is also good for you.

words.description: Plants or parts of plants eaten as food such as carrots and onions
words.example: Vegetables are good for you.

words.description: Long orange root vegetables
words.example: Carrots, beans, potatoes and peas are all vegetables.

words.description: Small seeds inside a pod, eaten as a vegetable
words.example: Carrots, beans, potatoes and peas are all vegetables.

words.description: Round starchy vegetables that grow underground
words.example: She made soup with potatoes, tomatoes and onions.

words.description: Red round fruit usually used as a vegetable in cooking
words.example: She put cheese and tomato in her sandwich.

words.description: Small round green seeds eaten as a vegetable
words.example: Carrots, beans, potatoes and peas are all vegetables.

words.description: Round vegetables with a strong taste, used in cooking
words.example: The soup has onions, garlic and mushrooms in it.

words.description: A plant with a strong smell and taste, used in cooking
words.example: The soup has onions, garlic and mushrooms in it.

words.description: A type of fungus eaten as a vegetable
words.example: The soup has onions, garlic and mushrooms in it.

words.description: A round fruit, red, yellow or green, with a sweet or sour taste
words.example: An apple a day keeps the doctor away.

words.description: A round orange citrus fruit with a sweet juice
words.example: I drink orange juice every morning.

words.description: A long curved yellow fruit
words.example: She had a banana for a snack.

words.description: A sweet juicy fruit that is narrow at the top and wider at the bottom
words.example: She ate a pear after lunch.

words.description: Small round green or purple fruits that grow in bunches
words.example: She bought a bunch of grapes.

words.description: Small red sweet fruits
words.example: I love strawberries and cream.

words.description: A large tropical fruit with a rough brown skin and sweet yellow flesh
words.example: She bought a pineapple from the market.

words.description: A white liquid produced by cows and other animals, used as a drink
words.example: Do you want milk in your tea?

words.description: A drink made from the liquid of crushed or squeezed fruit
words.example: I drink orange juice every morning.

words.description: An alcoholic drink made from grain
words.example: He ordered a beer at the pub.

words.description: An alcoholic drink made from grapes
words.example: She drank a glass of wine with dinner.

words.description: Water from a natural spring, often sold in bottles
words.example: I'll have a mineral water, please.

units.title: In the kitchen
units.topic: Kitchen appliances, utensils, and things used for cooking and eating

words.description: A machine that keeps food cold; short for refrigerator
words.example: Put the milk in the fridge.

words.description: A machine or compartment that freezes food at very low temperatures
words.example: The ice cream is in the freezer.

words.description: An oven that heats food very quickly using microwaves
words.example: Heat the soup in the microwave.

words.example: What size is this T-shirt? Small, medium or large?

words.description: The amount of money something costs
words.example: The price of the T-shirt is £6.99.

words.description: The visual quality of something such as blue, red or green; used when choosing items online
words.example: Available in three colours: white, blue and red.

words.description: A service where goods are delivered to you the day after you order them
words.example: Next Day Delivery costs £5.99.

words.description: A service where goods are delivered to you at no extra cost
words.example: Free Delivery takes 3-5 days.

words.description: A code at the end of a postal address that helps sort mail; called 'zip code' in the US
words.example: A post code is a long number or mix of letters at the end of your address.

words.description: To remove a product from your online shopping basket
words.example: Click 'Delete this item' to remove it from your basket.

words.description: The address where you want your online order to be sent
words.example: Please fill in your delivery address carefully.

units.title: In a hotel
units.topic: Hotel vocabulary, rooms, and useful expressions

words.description: The desk or area in a hotel where guests check in and out and get help
words.example: Please go to reception when you arrive.

words.description: A machine that carries people between floors of a building; 'elevator' in American English
words.example: Your room is on the first floor. Take the lift.

words.description: A metal or plastic card used to open a hotel room door
words.example: Here is your key. Your room is on the third floor.

words.description: A document showing how much money you owe for your hotel stay
words.example: Can I have the bill, please?

words.description: The bags and cases you bring with you to the hotel
words.example: Would you like some help with your luggage?

words.description: A document you fill in with your personal details when checking in
words.example: Can you fill in this form, please?

words.description: A hotel room with a single bed for one person
words.example: Do you have a single room available?

words.description: A hotel room with a double bed for two people
words.example: I'd like a double room for two nights.

words.description: A booking made in advance to guarantee a hotel room
words.example: I have a reservation. My name is Smith.

words.description: A hotel room that looks out onto something attractive like the sea or mountains
words.example: We'd like a room with a view of the sea.

words.description: To pay your bill and leave the hotel at the end of your stay
words.example: Can I check out, please?

words.description: Food and drinks delivered directly to your hotel room
words.example: Can I order room service?

words.description: A phone call from hotel reception to wake you at a specific time
words.example: Can I have a wake-up call at 6.30, please?

words.description: An electric device for drying hair with hot air
words.example: Is there a hairdryer in the room?

words.description: A small fridge in a hotel room stocked with drinks and snacks for purchase
words.example: The room has a mini-bar with cold drinks.

words.description: To write the required information in the spaces on a form
words.example: Can you fill in this form, please?

words.description: To write your name as a signature
words.example: Sign your name here, please.

words.description: To look carefully at the bill to make sure it is correct
words.example: Please check your bill to make sure it is correct.

words.description: Located on the floor above the ground floor
words.example: Your room is on the first floor.

words.description: At a place some distance away that you can point to
words.example: The lift is over there.

units.title: Eating out
units.topic: Places to eat, restaurant vocabulary and ordering food

words.description: A small restaurant where you can have coffee, tea, snacks and sometimes meals
words.example: You can have a cup of tea or coffee and a snack at a café.

words.description: A place where you pay to eat full meals; usually more expensive than a café
words.example: We went to a restaurant for dinner.

words.description: Places where alcohol and soft drinks are served; you can usually eat snacks or meals there too
words.example: Bars and pubs serve alcohol and soft drinks.

words.description: A restaurant where you get quick hot meals like burgers and chips
words.example: You can get a quick hot meal at a fast food restaurant.

words.description: Food you buy at a restaurant and take home to eat
words.example: I don't want to cook tonight. Let's get a take-away.

words.description: A list of food and drinks available in a restaurant
words.example: Can I see the menu, please?

words.description: Small dishes eaten at the beginning of a meal before the main course
words.example: For starters, I'll have the soup of the day.

words.description: The largest and most important dish of a meal
words.example: For the main course, I'd like the steak with chips.

words.description: A sweet food eaten at the end of a meal
words.example: For dessert, I'll have the chocolate ice cream.

words.description: A man who serves food and drinks in a restaurant
words.example: The waiter took our order.

words.description: What a waiter says when they come to take your food order
words.example: Are you ready to order? Yes, I'd like the steak, please.

words.description: A polite way to order or ask for something in a restaurant
words.example: I'd like the steak, please.

words.description: A polite question asking what someone wants
words.example: Would you like it with chips or new potatoes?

words.description: Words describing how much a steak is cooked
words.example: How would you like your steak — rare, medium or well-done?

words.description: A way to order food or drinks in a restaurant
words.example: I'll have a coke, please.

words.description: What a waiter asks to check if you are happy with your food
words.example: Is everything all right? Yes, it's delicious.

words.description: Having a very pleasant taste; extremely good to eat
words.example: Thank you, it's delicious.

words.description: A polite way to ask for the bill at the end of a meal
words.example: Could I have the bill, please?

words.description: Non-alcoholic drinks such as fruit juice and cola
words.example: Bars and pubs serve alcohol and soft drinks.

words.description: A dish that does not contain meat or fish; shown on menus with (v)
words.example: Vegetable curry (v) — this dish is vegetarian.

units.title: Sports
units.topic: Ball games, other sports, and sports vocabulary

words.description: A game played between two teams of 11 players who kick a round ball into a goal
words.example: We play football. I play football every weekend.

words.description: A team sport played with an oval ball that can be carried or kicked
words.example: We play rugby on the school pitch.

words.description: A team sport popular in the USA, played with an oval ball and helmets
words.example: American football is very popular in the USA.

words.description: A game where two teams try to throw a ball through a high hoop
words.example: We play basketball on the basketball court.

words.description: A game played with rackets and a shuttlecock over a net
words.example: We play badminton on the badminton court.

words.description: An American sport played with a bat and ball between two teams of nine players
words.example: Baseball is very popular in the United States.

words.description: A game played with rackets and a ball over a net on a court
words.example: We play tennis on the tennis court.

words.description: A game where two teams hit a ball over a high net with their hands
words.example: We play volleyball on the volleyball court.

words.description: A game like tennis but played on a table with small paddles and a light ball
words.example: I play table tennis with my friends.

words.description: The sport or activity of moving through water using your arms and legs
words.example: I go swimming every morning.

words.description: The sport or activity of running for exercise or competition
words.example: I go running every day.

words.description: The sport or activity of travelling on water in a boat with sails
words.example: I go sailing at the weekends.

words.description: The sport of racing cars at high speed on circuits
words.example: I like motor racing best.

words.description: The sport of racing horses ridden by jockeys
words.example: Horse racing is a popular sport in the UK.

words.description: Martial arts sports. Note: use 'do' with these — 'She does judo'
words.example: She does judo. He does karate.

words.description: The sport of sliding down snow-covered slopes on a snowboard
words.example: I went snowboarding last winter.

words.description: The sport of sliding down snow-covered slopes on skis
words.example: I went skiing last year.

words.description: The sport of paddling in a small narrow boat called a kayak
words.example: I go kayaking on the river.

words.description: Used with ball games and some other sports: football, tennis, basketball etc
words.example: Do you play football? I play tennis.

words.description: Used with sports ending in -ing: swimming, running, skiing, sailing etc. Note: NOT judo and karate
words.example: I go swimming. I go running every day.

words.description: Used with judo and karate
words.example: She does judo. He does karate.

words.description: The surface or area where tennis is played
words.example: We play tennis on a tennis court.

words.description: The grass area where football is played
words.example: We play football on a football pitch.

words.description: An artificial pool of water used for swimming
words.example: We swim in a swimming pool.

words.description: A building where many different sports and exercise activities can be done
words.example: Many towns have a sports centre where you can do lots of different sports.

units.title: Cinema
units.topic: Types of films and cinema vocabulary

words.description: A film set in the American West in the 19th century, usually with cowboys
words.example: Do you like westerns? No, I like science fiction films best.

words.description: An animated film, often made for children, with drawn or computer-generated characters
words.example: The children watch cartoons on Saturday mornings.

words.description: A film designed to frighten you with scary and disturbing scenes
words.example: If I see a horror film, I can't sleep.

words.description: A funny film or TV show intended to make people laugh
words.example: I love comedies because they make me laugh.

words.description: An exciting film with a lot of tension and suspense
words.example: I like watching thrillers because they are exciting.

words.description: A film with a lot of exciting scenes, fighting and adventure
words.example: The best action film I've seen was Jason Bourne.

words.description: A film set in the future or in space, often with robots or aliens
words.example: No, I like science fiction films best.

words.description: A film or show where characters sing and dance to tell the story
words.example: She loves musicals and knows all the songs.

words.description: A funny film about people falling in love; also called 'rom-com'
words.example: She loves watching romantic comedies.

words.description: A very famous and popular actor or actress in films
words.example: Monica Bellucci is a film star.

words.description: The person who is in charge of making a film and tells the actors what to do
words.example: I like films by Italian directors.

words.description: Acted the role of a character in a film or play
words.example: Daniel Radcliffe played Harry Potter in the Harry Potter films.

words.description: To appear as an actor in a film
words.example: She was in the new James Bond film.

words.description: Used to ask if someone has watched a particular film
words.example: Have you seen the latest James Bond film?

words.word: I loved it / I enjoyed it
words.description: Ways to say you liked a film very much
words.example: Yes, I loved it / I enjoyed it.

words.description: Not interesting; making you feel tired and uninterested
words.example: Did you like the new Batman film? No, it was boring.

words.description: A question asking which films are currently showing at the cinema
words.example: What's on at the cinema this week?

words.description: To visit a cinema to watch a film. Note: 'go to the cinema' NOT 'go to cinema'
words.example: Do you go to the cinema often? Yes, I go every week.

words.description: Watched a film on television rather than at the cinema
words.example: Have you seen the latest James Bond film? Yes, I saw it on TV.

units.title: Free time at home
units.topic: Hobbies and leisure activities at home

words.description: To view programmes on television. Note: 'watch TV' NOT 'see TV'
words.example: I watch TV every evening.

words.description: Shows or broadcasts on TV or radio
words.example: What programmes do you like best on TV?

words.description: To watch a film or show recorded on a DVD disc
words.example: At the weekend, we usually watch a DVD.

words.description: A pair of small speakers worn over or in your ears for listening to music or audio
words.example: I need some new headphones for my phone.

words.description: To enjoy music through speakers or headphones
words.example: I often listen to music on my phone when I'm relaxing.

words.description: To hear programmes broadcast on the radio
words.example: Sometimes I listen to the radio in the morning.

words.description: To play games on a console, computer or phone
words.example: A lot of young people play video games every day.

words.description: To copy music, films or other content from the internet to your device
words.example: I download music and films from the Internet.

words.description: To communicate with friends through social media or messaging apps
words.example: I chat to my friends online every evening.

words.description: The activity of preparing and cooking food as a hobby
words.example: I really like cooking.

words.description: The activity of growing and caring for plants in a garden
words.example: Do you like gardening?

words.description: To plant and care for flowers in your garden
words.example: We grow flowers and vegetables in our garden.

words.description: To read long fiction books for pleasure
words.example: I read novels — long stories. My sister likes comics.

words.description: Books or magazines with stories told through pictures and speech bubbles
words.example: My sister likes comics.

words.description: A daily or weekly publication containing news, articles and advertisements
words.example: Do you read a newspaper every day?

words.description: A weekly or monthly publication with articles on various topics
words.example: I like magazines about music and sport.

words.description: To invite friends to visit your home
words.example: We sometimes have friends round on Saturday evenings.

words.description: To invite friends to your home for an evening meal
words.example: I often have friends to dinner.

words.description: To visit someone and sleep at their home for one or more nights
words.example: My best friend comes to stay sometimes.

words.description: To speak to someone using a telephone
words.example: I talk to my friends on the phone every evening.

words.description: To relax completely without any activity
words.example: Sometimes I just do nothing.

words.description: To take a short sleep or nap during the day
words.example: Grandpa likes to have a sleep after lunch.

units.title: Music and musical instruments
units.topic: Types of music, musical instruments, and related vocabulary

words.description: Sounds arranged in a pleasing way. Note: uncountable — 'fantastic music' NOT 'fantastic musics'
words.example: The band played fantastic music for more than two hours.

words.description: Relating to music. Note: 'musical instruments' NOT 'music instruments'
words.example: There is a shop that sells musical instruments.

words.description: A person who plays a musical instrument or sings professionally
words.example: My brother is a very good musician. He plays three instruments.

words.description: A large keyboard instrument with black and white keys played by pressing them
words.example: She plays the piano beautifully.

words.description: A large stringed instrument played with a bow, held between the knees
words.example: He plays the cello in the school orchestra.

words.description: A stringed instrument with a body and neck, played by plucking or strumming
words.example: Krishnan is learning the guitar.

words.description: A wind instrument played by blowing across a hole at one end
words.example: Mia is a very good flute-player.

words.description: A small stringed instrument played with a bow held under the chin
words.example: Lucas is an excellent violinist.

words.description: A brass wind instrument with a flared bell, played by blowing
words.example: Nuria is a good trumpet-player.

words.description: A wind instrument with a single reed, played by blowing into one end
words.example: Ava plays the clarinet.

words.description: A percussion instrument played by hitting stretched skins with sticks. Always plural
words.example: Her brother plays the drums.

words.description: To perform on a musical instrument. Note: use 'the' with instruments — 'plays the guitar'
words.example: Can you play a musical instrument?

words.description: To be a member of a large group of musicians who play together
words.example: She plays in an orchestra.

words.description: To receive regular teaching from a music teacher on an instrument
words.example: His friend Isabella has piano lessons.

words.description: Serious traditional music composed by composers like Beethoven and Mozart. Note: 'classical music' NOT 'classic music'
words.example: Charlotte loves classical music — for example, Beethoven and Mozart.

words.description: A type of musical performance where singers perform a dramatic story
words.example: Harper can't stand opera. She prefers pop music.

words.description: Popular modern music with a strong beat and simple melodies
words.example: She prefers pop music.

words.description: Traditional music from a particular country or region
words.example: I like folk music, jazz and rock.

words.description: A style of music developed from blues and ragtime, known for improvisation
words.example: I like folk music, jazz and rock.

words.description: A type of loud popular music with a strong beat, usually with electric guitars
words.example: I like folk music, jazz and rock.

words.description: A group of musicians who play together. Note: 'a band' NOT 'a music band'
words.example: A band means a group of musicians.

words.description: A live musical performance. Note: 'a concert' NOT 'a music concert'
words.example: We're going to a concert tonight.

words.description: To enjoy music through speakers or headphones
words.example: I often listen to music on the train.

words.description: To copy songs from the internet to your device
words.example: I downloaded some new songs yesterday.

units.title: Countries and nationalities
units.topic: Continents, countries, nationalities and languages

words.description: One of the seven large land masses on Earth. Note: continents always begin with a capital letter
words.example: Uzbekistan is in the continent of Asia.

words.description: The continent that includes Canada, the USA and Mexico
words.example: Canada and the USA are in North America.

words.description: The continent that includes Brazil, Argentina, Chile and other countries
words.example: Brazil and Argentina are in South America.

words.description: The continent that includes the UK, Germany, France, Spain and other countries
words.example: Germany, Italy and Spain are in Europe.

words.description: The largest continent, including China, India, Japan, Uzbekistan and others
words.example: China, India and Japan are in Asia.

words.description: The second largest continent, including Egypt, Morocco and South Africa
words.example: Egypt and Morocco are in Africa.

words.description: Both a continent and a country in the southern hemisphere
words.example: Australia and New Zealand are in Australia.

words.description: The frozen continent around the South Pole
words.example: Antarctica is the coldest continent on Earth.

words.description: The status of belonging to a particular country. Note: always begins with a capital letter
words.example: My nationality is Uzbek — I am from Uzbekistan.

words.description: Relating to or from the United States of America
words.example: She is American. She comes from New York.

words.description: Relating to or from Great Britain (England, Scotland, Wales)
words.example: He is British. He comes from London.

words.description: Relating to or from China. Note: adjectives ending in -ese
words.example: She is Chinese. She speaks Chinese.

words.description: Relating to or from Japan. Note: adjectives ending in -ese
words.example: He is Japanese. He speaks Japanese.

words.description: Relating to or from Spain. Note: adjectives ending in -ish
words.example: She is Spanish. She speaks Spanish.

words.description: Relating to or from France. Note: exception — does not end in -an, -ish or -ese
words.example: He is French. He speaks French.

words.description: Relating to or from Germany. Note: adjectives ending in -an
words.example: She is German. She speaks German.

words.description: Relating to or from Brazil
words.example: He is Brazilian. He speaks Portuguese.

words.description: A system of communication used by people of a country or community
words.example: The official language of Brazil is Portuguese.

words.description: The language spoken in Arab countries. Note: the language is Arabic, not Arab
words.example: She speaks Arabic. French, Spanish, Japanese and Thai share the language adjective.

words.description: An uppercase letter. Note: all nouns and adjectives for continents, countries and nationalities always begin with a capital letter
words.example: All the nouns and adjectives for countries and nationalities begin with a capital letter.

units.title: Weather
units.topic: Types of weather, weather adjectives and useful expressions

words.description: The star that gives light and warmth to the Earth
words.example: The sun is shining in Granada.

words.description: Having a lot of sunshine; bright and warm. Note: 'it's sunny' NOT 'it's sunning'
words.example: It's a sunny day in Tokyo.

words.description: Water that falls from clouds in the sky
words.example: It can be very wet in London — carry an umbrella.

words.description: Having a lot of rain
words.example: It's a rainy day in London.

words.description: The present continuous used to describe rain currently falling
words.example: It's raining in Barcelona.

words.description: A white or grey mass of water vapour floating in the sky
words.example: There are dark clouds in the sky.

words.description: Having a lot of clouds in the sky; overcast. Note: 'it's cloudy' NOT 'it's clouding'
words.example: It's a bit cloudy in Hong Kong today.

words.description: White frozen water that falls from clouds in cold weather
words.example: It's snowing in Moscow.

words.description: Covered with snow or having a lot of snow falling
words.example: It's a snowy day in the mountains.

words.description: Thick cloud close to the ground that makes it hard to see
words.example: There is thick fog on the motorway.

words.description: Having thick cloud close to the ground. Note: 'it's foggy' NOT 'it's fogging'
words.example: It's foggy in Sydney.

words.description: Air moving quickly outdoors
words.example: There is a strong wind blowing today.

words.description: Having strong winds blowing. Note: 'it's windy' NOT 'it's winding'
words.example: It's very windy on the coast.

words.description: The loud sound heard after lightning during a storm
words.example: I could hear thunder in the distance.

words.description: Having or likely to have thunder
words.example: It's thundery weather today.

words.description: A bright flash of light in the sky during a storm. Note: no adjective form
words.example: There was lightning and thunder all night.

words.description: Having a very high temperature
words.example: It is very hot in Mexico — often 45 degrees in summer.

words.description: Having a very low temperature
words.example: It is very cold in the Arctic — often minus 50 degrees.

words.description: Having a lot of rain; not dry
words.example: It can be very wet in London.

words.description: Having little or no rain
words.example: It is very dry in the Sahara — it doesn't often rain there.

words.description: A very strong violent storm with extremely fast winds
words.example: A hurricane is a very strong wind.

words.description: A period of very bad weather with strong winds and heavy rain
words.example: A storm is when there is a strong wind and rain together.

words.description: A storm with thunder, lightning, heavy rain and sometimes wind
words.example: There was a terrible thunderstorm last night.

words.description: A question asking about current weather conditions
words.example: What's the weather like in your country in June?

words.description: Used to describe bright sunny weather
words.example: The sun is shining in Granada.

units.title: In the town
units.topic: Places in a town, notices, and asking for directions

words.description: A place where trains stop to let people get on and off
words.example: Train station — you can get a train here.

words.description: A place where buses start and finish their journeys
words.example: Bus station — you can get a bus here.

words.description: A large building or area with many shops inside
words.example: Shopping centre — area of town with a lot of shops.

words.description: A place where visitors can get information about the local area
words.example: Tourist information office — tourists can get information here.

words.description: A building where interesting historical or artistic objects are kept and displayed
words.example: Museum — you can see interesting old things here.

words.description: A place where you can keep, borrow or exchange money
words.example: Bank — you can change money here or get money from a cash machine.

words.description: A place where you can post letters and parcels
words.example: Post office — you can post letters and parcels here.

words.description: A place where books and newspapers are kept and can be borrowed for free
words.example: Library — you can read books and newspapers here.

words.description: The main local government building in a town
words.example: Town hall — local government officers work here.

words.description: An area or building where cars can be left temporarily
words.example: Car park — you can park your car here.

words.description: A part of town where only people on foot are allowed; no cars
words.example: Pedestrian area — you can only walk here, you can't come here by car.

words.description: A machine from which you can withdraw cash using a bank card
words.example: Bank — you can change money here or get money from a cash machine.

words.description: A sign showing the way out of a building
words.example: Follow the EXIT signs to leave the building.

words.description: A sign at the way into a building
words.example: The Entrance to the museum is on the left.

words.description: A sign prohibiting smoking in an area
words.example: There is a NO SMOKING sign in the waiting room.

words.description: A sign indicating that a machine is not working
words.example: The lift is OUT OF ORDER. Please use the stairs.

words.description: A question asking for the location of a place
words.example: Where is the bus station?

words.description: An instruction to turn in the left direction
words.example: Go left here and it's at the end of the road.

words.description: An instruction to turn right at the first junction
words.example: Take the first right and then the second left.

words.description: An instruction to turn in the right direction
words.example: Turn right here. The entrance is on Market Street on the left.

words.word: on the left / on the right
words.description: Used to describe the position of something in relation to a road
words.example: The entrance is on Market Street on the left.

words.word: at the end of the road
words.description: Located at the far end of a street
words.example: Go left here and it's at the end of the road.

words.word: on the other side of the road
words.transcription: /ɒn ðɪ ˈʌðər saɪd əv ðə roʊd/
words.description: On the opposite side of the street
words.example: Can you tell me the way to the nearest bank? There's one on the other side of the road.

units.title: In the countryside
units.topic: Natural features, countryside vocabulary, and nature

words.description: Land outside towns and cities; rural areas with fields, farms and natural scenery. Note: 'I like walking in the countryside' NOT 'in the nature'
words.example: The countryside and the country both mean 'not the city'.

words.description: Very high areas of land with steep sides and peaks
words.example: You can go walking or skiing in the mountains.

words.description: Areas of high land that are smaller and less steep than mountains
words.example: The hills in the background are very beautiful.

words.description: A large area of land covered with trees
words.example: There is a large forest to the north of the town.

words.description: A small area of trees; smaller than a forest
words.example: There is a small wood at the end of the village.

words.description: A large area of water surrounded by land
words.example: We went swimming in the lake.

words.description: A large natural flow of water that runs into the sea or a lake
words.example: The river often floods after heavy rain.

words.description: Large areas of flat open land, usually used for farming
words.example: A farmer lives on a farm and works in the fields.

words.description: A narrow track for people to walk along
words.example: We followed the path through the woods.

words.description: A narrow road in the countryside
words.example: Be careful driving on country roads — they are narrow.

words.description: A small community of houses and people, smaller than a town
words.example: In the countryside, people usually live in a small town or village.

words.description: A place smaller than a city but larger than a village, usually with about 6,000 people
words.example: In the countryside, people usually live in a small town or village.

words.description: An area of land with buildings where crops are grown and animals are kept
words.example: A farmer lives on a farm and works in the fields.

words.description: A person who owns or manages a farm
words.example: A farmer lives on a farm and works in the fields.

words.description: A small house in a village or the countryside
words.example: My friend lives in a cottage in the countryside.

words.description: Everything in the natural world including animals, birds, plants and landscapes. Note: 'I love nature' NOT 'I love the nature'
words.example: Nature means everything in the natural world.

words.description: Animals, birds, fish and insects living in their natural environment
words.example: There is wonderful wildlife in the north of the country.

words.description: A place where wildlife and nature are officially protected
words.example: Near the village there is a conservation area.

words.description: A very large area of beautiful natural land protected by the government
words.example: In the south of the country there is a national park.

words.description: To eat a meal outdoors in the countryside or a park
words.example: You can take food and drink and have a picnic.

words.description: To walk in the countryside for pleasure or exercise
words.example: You can go walking or skiing in the mountains.
